summary: Iteration 2 testing — W.A.I. rebrand + Phase C (Digital Credentialing + Portfolio Builder). Backend: 49/49 pytest tests PASSED (100%) including 14 new credential/portfolio/rebrand tests + 35 regression tests. Frontend: All critical flows verified — landing/login/sidebar rebrand to W.A.I. with logo, blue (#1E5BA8) palette, 'CURRENT ASSOCIATE' card; /credentials page with Earned (1) + Available (13) tabs, OpenBadges manifest + assertion external links; /portfolio page with publish/copy/export PDF; public /p/{slug} view (no auth) shows portfolio WITHOUT email or publish panel. Found and FIXED one critical frontend bug (Portfolio page crashed with 'destroy is not a function' due to useEffect(load, [])).

minor.issue: LabSubmitReq still requires lab_slug in body even though slug is in URL path (carried over from iteration 1).

issues: Garbled certificate preview image still present from iteration 1 — not retested but unchanged.

action_items: (Already fixed by testing agent) Portfolio.jsx useEffect bug — useEffect(load, []) returned a Promise, causing 'destroy is not a function' runtime crash. Fixed by wrapping in arrow: useEffect(() => { load(); }, []).
Optional: Make LabSubmitReq.lab_slug optional or auto-derive from path slug.
Optional: Replace garbled certificate preview image asset.
Optional: Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md from 'Cohort' to 'Associate' (still says 'Associate-Alpha' — already done in iter 1).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1196 lines — exceeded 700-line guideline by ~500 lines. Strongly recommend splitting into routers: routers/auth.py, routers/modules.py, routers/labs.py, routers/credentials.py, routers/portfolio.py, routers/instructor.py, routers/admin.py.
build_portfolio() at server.py:979 does N+1 queries (per-submission db.labs.find_one). Should batch-fetch labs by slug list once.

PortfolioPublishReq.publish defaults likely None — verify with explicit type. Slug uses user.full_name.lower().replace(' ', '-') without checking collisions across users with same name (relies on user.id[:8] suffix — acceptable).

Portfolio.jsx had useEffect(load, []) returning a Promise — this is a recurring React anti-pattern. Lint rule react-hooks/exhaustive-deps + no-misused-promises would catch it. Recommend adding ESLint plugin react-hooks if not present.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_lcewai.py (added TestCredentials, TestPortfolio, TestRebrand classes — 14 new tests)
/app/frontend/src/pages/Portfolio.jsx (fixed useEffect to not return a Promise)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of critical flows (after the Portfolio fix)

seed_data_creation: Tests register TEST_<uuid>@example.com students and award credentials on the seeded student account. Portfolio publish leaves student in 'published' state with slug 'alex-carter-bc4155ad' (or similar) — non-destructive. No cleanup needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_lcewai.py cover 49 cases across auth/modules/quiz/certs/AI/labs/instructor/competencies/admin/credentials/portfolio/rebrand. Student account auto-earns 'osha-10-awareness' credential after completing safety-loto quiz. Portfolio publish endpoint creates slug pattern '{full_name-kebab}-{user_id[:8]}'. Public portfolio strips email field. The fix to Portfolio.jsx (useEffect wrapping) should NOT be reverted. Manifest endpoint is public (no auth). Assertion endpoint also public. Both return OpenBadges v2 JSON.
rca of the issue: Portfolio page crash RCA — useEffect(load, []) where load is `() => api.get(...).then(...)` returns a Promise. React useEffect must return either undefined or a cleanup function. React in dev mode invokes the returned value as a destroy function on unmount, throwing TypeError: destroy is not a function. The crash showed an 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay covering the page so no portfolio UI rendered. Fix: wrap in `useEffect(() => { load(); }, [])`. Verified post-fix: Portfolio renders correctly, publish/copy/export-pdf all work, public view at /p/{slug} loads in a fresh browser context without auth and without leaking email.
